neighbor:
A hash table achieves average O(1) lookup by using a hash function to map keys to indices in an array. When a key is inserted or looked up, the hash function is applied to it, and the resulting index is used to access the corresponding value in the array.

The key to achieving average O(1) lookup is the use of a good hash function. A good hash function should distribute keys uniformly across the array, minimizing the number of collisions (when two keys hash to the same index). Collisions can be resolved using a collision resolution strategy, such as chaining or open addressing.

Chaining involves storing a linked list of key-value pairs at each index in the array. When
